title: NZ School Holidays Travel Tips — How to Beat the Crowds and Save Money
meta_desc: Discover how to save money and avoid crowds during NZ school holidays with expert travel tips and insider knowledge
intro: If you're planning a family trip during the NZ school holidays, be prepared for crowds and hefty price tags, with some popular attractions like the Skyline Gondola in Queenstown increasing their prices by up to 20% during peak season. However, with some smart planning and insider knowledge, you can beat the crowds and save money. In this article, we'll share our top tips for navigating the NZ school holidays like a pro.

sections.heading: Plan Ahead and Book Early
sections.body: One of the most effective ways to save money and avoid crowds during the NZ school holidays is to plan ahead and book early. Popular attractions and accommodations tend to fill up quickly, so it's essential to book at least 2-3 months in advance to avoid missing out. For example, booking a campsite at the popular Lake Tekapo Holiday Park can cost around $40-$60 per night during peak season, but booking early can secure a spot for as low as $30-$40 per night.

sections.heading: Alternative Destinations
list: Consider visiting lesser-known destinations like the Catlins, which offers stunning coastal scenery and wildlife spotting opportunities
Head to the South Island's West Coast, which is often less crowded than the East Coast
Explore the beautiful Coromandel Peninsula, which offers a range of outdoor activities and stunning beaches

sections.heading: Drive Times and Transportation
sections.body: When planning your trip, it's essential to consider drive times and transportation options. For example, driving from Auckland to Wellington can take around 8-10 hours, depending on traffic and road conditions. Consider breaking up the journey with stops in destinations like Taupo or Palmerston North, which can help to reduce fatigue and make the journey more enjoyable. You can also use our NZ Family Travel Budget Calculator to estimate fuel costs and plan your route.

sections.heading: Accommodation Options
sections.body: When it comes to accommodation, there are a range of options to suit all budgets and preferences. Consider staying in a campervan, which can be a cost-effective and flexible option, with prices starting from around $100-$150 per day. You can also stay in a holiday park or motel, which can offer a range of amenities and services. For more information on accommodation options, check out our Campervans page or Destinations page.

sections.heading: Get the Most Out of Your Trip

cta.text: Use our NZ Family Travel Budget Calculator to plan your trip

sections.heading: Conclusion
sections.body: With some smart planning and insider knowledge, you can beat the crowds and save money during the NZ school holidays. Remember to plan ahead, book early, and consider alternative destinations and accommodation options. Don't forget to use our NZ Family Travel Budget Calculator to estimate costs and plan your route. Happy travels!
